content:
I am going to say the thing Go developers whisper and Go haters shout.

6 min read
Just now

--

Go is not a beautiful language. It was never even trying to be one.
Go won because it made a bet no other modern language had the nerve to make. It bet that your team is average, and it built every single design decision on top of that bet.
If you’re not a Medium member, this friend link gets you the full article free — bring your counterarguments.
Before you close this tab in rage, understand something. I mean this as the highest compliment a language can receive.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Quote Everyone Misreads
Rob Pike said it out loud years ago. He described Google’s engineers as young, fresh out of school, and not ready for a brilliant language.
The internet dunked on that quote for a decade. It became proof that Go was condescending, a language built for people who could not handle real power.
Almost nobody noticed what Pike actually did. He described every engineering team on the planet, accurately, for the first time in language design history.
